dominant_palette: yellow gold, jet black, warm white/ivory, deep ruby red, silver-platinum
secondary_palette: teal-pavé, blush-pink, tanzanite blue-violet, forest green
recurring_motifs: star (as cut stone and as ring form)
composition_vocabulary: Consistently object-centred: the jewel fills roughly two-thirds of the tile against a clean or deliberately dark neutral ground. A minority of tiles layer in reference sheets, branded type, or pop-cultural ephemera, introducing a graphic-design register without breaking the product-centred frame.
lighting_quality: Predominantly controlled studio — hard directional light that fires up facets and metal surfaces; roughly a third of the sample shifts to dark-background chiaroscuro that flattens the setting and lets the piece glow in isolation.
material_textures: polished yellow gold, pavé diamond, smooth faceted gemstone, coloured enamel, baroque pearl, oxidised or dark-plated metal
aesthetic_register: The sample occupies a luxury-talisman register — fine-jewellery fabrication and gemstone weight anchoring pieces that are densely symbolic (serpents, fangs, claws, celestial forms), with occasional K-pop and graphic-design intrusions signalling contemporary cultural self-awareness rather than pure heirloom traditionalism.
notably_absent: On-body or lifestyle context — no hands wearing rings, no draped necks or styled ears; the curator is collecting object-studies and symbolic ideas, not aspirational wearability or human warmth.
